Here's a paragraph about Arctostaphylos uva-ursi (bearberry):

Arctostaphylos uva-ursi, commonly known as bearberry, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to Northern Hemisphere boreal forests. Bearberry has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including treating urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and digestive issues due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of bearberry include arbutin, hydroquinone, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
